message:
refactor(streaming): eliminate redundant partial-clone from StreamEvent variants

Remove the `partial: AssistantMessage` field from all content-level
StreamEvent variants (TextStart/Delta/End, ThinkingStart/Delta/End,
ToolCallStart/Delta/End). Previously every provider cloned the entire
accumulated AssistantMessage on each streaming event, causing O(n^2)
allocations proportional to response length. The agent loop now mutates
the message in-place via `self.messages.last_mut()` and only clones when
emitting AgentEvent callbacks, which already required a clone anyway.

Key changes across the codebase:

**model.rs**: Stripped `partial` field from 9 StreamEvent variants,
leaving only content_index + delta/content/tool_call as needed.

**agent.rs**: Rewrote the streaming event handler to:
- Mutate the in-progress AssistantMessage directly for Text, Thinking,
  and ToolCall content blocks instead of replacing with partial snapshots
- Track `sent_start` flag to avoid redundant MessageStart emissions
- Import ThinkingContent for in-place thinking block construction
- Add AutoCompactionStart/End and AutoRetryStart/End lifecycle events
  for observability into automatic error recovery

**All providers** (anthropic, azure, bedrock, cohere, gemini, gitlab,
openai, openai_responses, vertex, mod.rs):
- Remove `.partial: self.partial.clone()` from all event emissions
- Ensure proper Start → TextStart → TextDelta → TextEnd event sequences
  (azure, bedrock, gitlab, mod.rs gained missing lifecycle events)
- Fix TextEnd emission to cover ALL open text blocks, not just the last
  one (gemini, openai, azure) — text blocks preceding tool calls were
  previously left without TextEnd
- ExtensionStreamSimpleProvider gains buffered pending_events queue and
  proper string_chunk_started tracking for complete event lifecycle

**Provider proptest coverage** (anthropic, gemini): Added FUZZ-P1.3
process_event() property-based fuzz tests covering valid event sequences,
chaos JSON, and multi-event sequences to verify no panics occur under
arbitrary input.

**Tests**: Updated all event_label() match arms across 7 test files to
handle new AutoCompaction/AutoRetry variants. Updated provider streaming
tests and fixtures to match the new event shape (no more partial field).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -74,6 +74,42 @@ fn format_event(event: &AgentEvent) -> serde_json::Value {
             "tool_name": tool_name,
             "is_error": is_error,
         }),
+        AgentEvent::AutoCompactionStart { reason } => {
+            json!({ "event": "auto_compaction_start", "reason": reason })
+        }
+        AgentEvent::AutoCompactionEnd {
+            aborted,
+            will_retry,
+            error_message,
+            ..
+        } => json!({
+            "event": "auto_compaction_end",
+            "aborted": aborted,
+            "willRetry": will_retry,
+            "errorMessage": error_message,
+        }),
+        AgentEvent::AutoRetryStart {
+            attempt,
+            max_attempts,
+            delay_ms,
+            error_message,
+        } => json!({
+            "event": "auto_retry_start",
+            "attempt": attempt,
+            "maxAttempts": max_attempts,
+            "delayMs": delay_ms,
+            "errorMessage": error_message,
+        }),
+        AgentEvent::AutoRetryEnd {
+            success,
+            attempt,
+            final_error,
+        } => json!({
+            "event": "auto_retry_end",
+            "success": success,
+            "attempt": attempt,
+            "finalError": final_error,
+        }),
     }
 }
